title: Perityle lemmonii
intro: Perityle lemmonii, also known as Lemmon's marigold or Lemmon's perityle, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may contribute to the treatment of various conditions, including skin irritations and infections. The bioactive constituents of Perityle lemmonii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Perityle lemmonii is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly employed for their medicinal and therapeutic applications.
taxonomy: Perityle lemmonii is classified within the family Asteraceae. This plant belongs to the genus Perityle. Its classification also includes being a member of the tribe Heliantheae. Furthermore, it is a part of the subfamily Asteroideae. Perityle lemmonii's classification is significant for its understanding in the field of botany.
uses: Perityle lemmonii has been reported to have several medicinal uses, primarily based on its traditional applications by the indigenous people of the Sonoran Desert region. The plant's leaves and stems are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in treating various health issues. Perityle lemmonii is also used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and upset stomach. Additionally, it is thought to possess antimicrobial properties, making it effective against certain types of bacterial and fungal infections. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of Perityle lemmonii for medicinal purposes.
